subtopics.name: P1: Quadratic Solving & Vertex
learning_outcomes: Carry out the process of completing the square for a quadratic polynomial ax^2 + bx + c and use a completed square form[cite: 92].
Solve quadratic equations, and quadratic inequalities, in one unknown[cite: 94].
Recognise and solve equations in x which are quadratic in some function of x[cite: 96].
Locate the vertex of the graph of y = ax^2 + bx + c or sketch the graph[cite: 98].

subtopics.name: P1: Roots & Intersections
learning_outcomes: Find the discriminant of a quadratic polynomial ax^2 + bx + c and use the discriminant[cite: 93].
Determine the number of real roots of the equation ax^2 + bx + c = 0[cite: 99].
Solve by substitution a pair of simultaneous equations of which one is linear and one is quadratic[cite: 95].
Determine the set of values of k for which a line intersects, touches, or does not meet a quadratic curve[cite: 138].

subtopics.name: P1: Function Ops & Inverses
learning_outcomes: Understand the terms function, domain, range, one-one function, inverse function and composition of functions[cite: 111].
Identify the range of a given function and find the composition of two functions[cite: 112].
Determine whether a function is one-one and find the inverse of a one-one function[cite: 113].

subtopics.name: P1: Graph Transforms
learning_outcomes: Illustrate in graphical terms the relation between a one-one function and its inverse, including the mirror line y = x[cite: 114, 122].
Understand and use transformations: f(x) + a, f(x + a), af(x), f(ax) and their combinations[cite: 115, 116, 117].
Use terms 'translation', 'reflection' and 'stretch' in describing transformations[cite: 123].

subtopics.name: P1: Linear Coordinate Geometry
learning_outcomes: Find the equation of a straight line given two points or one point and the gradient[cite: 127, 133].
Interpret and use forms y = mx + c, y - y1 = m(x - x1), and ax + by + c = 0[cite: 128].
Use the relationship between the gradients of parallel and perpendicular lines[cite: 134].

subtopics.name: P1: Circle Geometry
learning_outcomes: Understand that the equation (x - a)^2 + (y - b)^2 = r^2 represents a circle[cite: 129].
Use expanded form x^2 + y^2 + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0[cite: 135].
Solve problems involving lines and circles using algebraic methods and elementary geometric properties[cite: 130, 136].

subtopics.name: P1: Circular Measure
learning_outcomes: Understand the definition of a radian and the relationship between radians and degrees[cite: 146].
Use formulae s = rθ and A = 0.5r^2θ for arc length and sector area[cite: 147].

subtopics.name: P1: Trig Ratios & Graphs
learning_outcomes: Sketch and use graphs of sine, cosine, and tangent functions for any size angle[cite: 152].
Use exact values of trigonometric functions for 30, 45, and 60 degrees[cite: 153].
Use notation for inverse trigonometric relations (principal values)[cite: 154].

subtopics.name: P1: Trig Identities & Eqns
learning_outcomes: Use identities sinθ/cosθ = tanθ and sin^2θ + cos^2θ = 1[cite: 156, 157].
Find all solutions of simple trigonometrical equations in a specified interval[cite: 159].

subtopics.name: P1: Binomial Expansion
learning_outcomes: Use the expansion of (a + b)^n where n is a positive integer[cite: 170].
Use the notation n! and the binomial coefficient formula[cite: 174, 175].

subtopics.name: P1: Progressions
learning_outcomes: Recognise arithmetic and geometric progressions[cite: 171].
Use formulae for the nth term and sum of n terms for both progression types[cite: 171].
Use the condition for convergence and the sum to infinity for geometric progressions[cite: 172].

subtopics.name: P1: Derivative Techniques
learning_outcomes: Understand the gradient as a limit and use derivative notation f'(x), f''(x), dy/dx, and d^2y/dx^2[cite: 187, 188].
Use the derivative of x^n and the chain rule for composite functions[cite: 191].

subtopics.name: P1: Differentiation Apps
learning_outcomes: Apply differentiation to gradients, tangents, normals, and increasing/decreasing functions[cite: 192].
Solve problems involving rates of change and connected rates of change[cite: 192, 198].
Locate stationary points, determine their nature (max/min), and use them in sketching[cite: 193, 199].

subtopics.name: P1: Integration Techniques
learning_outcomes: Understand integration as the reverse of differentiation and integrate (ax + b)^n[cite: 204].
Solve problems involving the evaluation of a constant of integration[cite: 205].
Evaluate definite and improper integrals[cite: 206, 214].

subtopics.name: P1: Areas & Volumes
learning_outcomes: Find the area of a region bounded by a curve and lines or between two curves[cite: 208].
Calculate a volume of revolution about one of the axes[cite: 209].

subtopics.name: S1: Data Visualisation
learning_outcomes: Select suitable ways of presenting raw data and discuss advantages/disadvantages[cite: 13, 14].
Draw and interpret stem-and-leaf diagrams (including back-to-back), box-and-whisker plots, histograms, and cumulative frequency graphs[cite: 15, 18].

subtopics.name: S1: Summary Statistics
learning_outcomes: Understand and use measures of central tendency: mean, median, and mode[cite: 16].
Understand and use measures of variation: range, IQR, and standard deviation[cite: 16].
Calculate mean and standard deviation from data, totals, or coded totals[cite: 21].

subtopics.name: S1: Permutations & Combinations
learning_outcomes: Solve simple selection problems using permutations and combinations[cite: 24].
Solve arrangement problems in a line involving repetition or restrictions[cite: 25, 26].

subtopics.name: S1: Probability Laws
learning_outcomes: Evaluate probabilities via enumeration or permutations/combinations[cite: 37].
Use addition and multiplication of probabilities[cite: 38].
Understand exclusive and independent events and test for independence[cite: 39].

subtopics.name: S1: Conditional Probability
learning_outcomes: Calculate and use conditional probabilities[cite: 40].
Use the conditional probability formula P(A|B) = P(A ∩ B) / P(B) in simple cases[cite: 46].

subtopics.name: S1: Discrete Distributions
learning_outcomes: Draw up probability distribution tables and calculate E(X) and Var(X)[cite: 49].

subtopics.name: S1: Binomial & Geo Models
learning_outcomes: Use formulae for binomial B(n, p) and geometric Geo(p) distributions[cite: 50, 53].
Recognise practical situations for these models and use expectation/variance formulae[cite: 50, 51].

subtopics.name: S1: Normal Distribution
learning_outcomes: Use a normal distribution to model a continuous random variable and use tables[cite: 62].
Solve problems using standardisation Z = (X - μ) / σ[cite: 63, 70].

subtopics.name: S1: Normal Approximation
learning_outcomes: Recall conditions (np > 5 and nq > 5) for using the normal distribution as an approximation to the binomial[cite: 66, 71].
Use the normal approximation with a continuity correction to solve problems[cite: 66].
